message:
feat(ft-xbnl0.2.3): cx-first timeouts in intelligent scheduler (tick 297)

Migrates 2 ambient `timeout` call sites in the snapshot engine's
intelligent-scheduling mode (scheduler_body L1014 + L1027) to
`timeout_with_cx`:

- L1019 shutdown-flag non-blocking poll:
  `timeout(Duration::ZERO, shutdown.changed(cx))` →
  `timeout_with_cx(cx, Duration::ZERO, shutdown.changed(cx))`
- L1036 trigger-receive wait:
  `timeout(wait_step, trigger_rx.recv(cx))` →
  `timeout_with_cx(cx, wait_step, trigger_rx.recv(cx))`

Both inner futures were already cx-aware (`shutdown.changed(cx)` +
`trigger_rx.recv(cx)`). Previously the outer `timeout` was ambient —
effectively cancel propagated through the inner future but the
timeout itself wasn't gated on cx. Now both layers are cx-first at
every await boundary.

Also drops unused `timeout` import from `runtime_compat::{...}` — all
timeout call sites in snapshot_engine.rs now use `timeout_with_cx`
via the crate path (`crate::runtime_compat::timeout_with_cx`).

Combined with tick 296 (periodic scheduler shutdown wait), both
periodic AND intelligent scheduling modes are strictly cx-cancel
responsive at every await boundary.

Verification: `cargo check -p frankenterm --bin ft` compiles clean
(7 warnings, baseline restored after dropping unused import).

Cumulative ft-xbnl0.2.3 ticks this session: 111 (187-297).
frankenterm-core internal sites: 58 (prior 56 + snapshot_engine 2).
main.rs cross-crate total: 192 sites (unchanged this tick).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
